title: AI Product Engineering
source: Authored for the Salareen corporate catalog; structured to mirror the UK apprenticeship standard format.

knowledge.K1: How AI products differ from deterministic software: probabilistic behaviour and designing for uncertainty.
knowledge.K2: Foundation models as building blocks accessed via APIs, including their capabilities, limits and cost.
knowledge.K3: Prompt engineering and structured output, treating prompts as versioned, tested code.
knowledge.K4: Retrieval-augmented generation to ground features in your data.
knowledge.K5: Building agents with tool use and appropriate guardrails.
knowledge.K7: Evaluating AI features against realistic test sets for quality and safety.
knowledge.K8: Productionising AI (LLMOps): versioning, cost, latency, fallbacks and monitoring.
knowledge.K9: Designing AI UX for imperfection: sources, correction and human control.
knowledge.K10: Responsible AI and safety: hallucination, bias, privacy and prompt injection.
knowledge.K12: Software engineering foundations applied to AI features.

skills.S1: Design AI features that handle variability and uncertainty.
skills.S2: Select and integrate foundation models for a feature.
skills.S3: Engineer reliable prompts with structured output.
skills.S4: Implement retrieval to ground features in data.
skills.S5: Build agents with bounded tools and guardrails.
skills.S6: Adapt or fine-tune models when justified.
skills.S7: Build evaluation sets and score features rigorously.
skills.S8: Operate AI features with LLMOps discipline.
skills.S9: Design AI UX that surfaces sources and enables correction.
skills.S11: Start from a user problem and measure value.
skills.S12: Apply software engineering practices to AI feature delivery.

behaviours.B1: Innovates with emerging AI through self-directed learning.
behaviours.B2: Takes responsibility for feature reliability and safety.
behaviours.B3: Collaborates across product, design and engineering.
behaviours.B4: Acts with integrity on AI ethics, safety and privacy.
behaviours.B5: Operates effectively amid technical complexity and uncertainty.

Duty 1.statement: Design AI-powered features for real user problems.

Duty 2.statement: Build features on foundation models with prompts and retrieval.

Duty 3.statement: Add agents and model adaptation where justified.

Duty 5.statement: Productionise and operate AI features reliably.

Duty 6.statement: Ship responsibly with safe, well-designed UX.
